title: ADK-301: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Orchestration
description: Design and orchestrate multi-agent collaboration environments. Configure A2AServer routing, launch remote agents, and write discovery card manifests.

highlights.title: A2AServer Routing
highlights.description: Master the deployment of central routing servers for multi-agent networking and task delegation.

highlights.title: Remote Bindings
highlights.description: Register agents hosted on remote endpoints with your orchestration network as local services.

highlights.title: Agent Manifests
highlights.description: Author agent-card.json files containing dynamic capability and scope declarations.

blocks.content: Multi-Agent Systems & Architecture Topologies

Complex software engineering problems cannot be solved efficiently by a single model loop. Splitting tasks across focused, specialized agents increases precision, reduces input context usage, and enables parallel processing pipelines.
Multi-Agent Topology Models
Developers can configure agent communications using two primary structure topologies:

items.title: Hub-and-Spoke Topology
items.content: A central coordinator agent receives user input, plans steps, delegating tasks to sub-agents, compiling output.

items.title: Peer-to-Peer Mesh Topology
items.content: Agents communicate directly, triggering callbacks and passing payloads autonomously based on state changes.

blocks.content: The A2A Protocol Architecture

The Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol defines how agents declare parameters, register endpoints, establish trust, and exchange structured JSON task payloads across local networks or public HTTP transports.
Check Your Understanding
Question 1: Which multi-agent topology uses a coordinator agent to process user inputs and delegate tasks?

- A) Peer-to-Peer Mesh
- B) Hub-and-Spoke
- C) Circular Chain
Feedback: Option B is correct. In a Hub-and-Spoke model, a central coordinator coordinates routing and delegation between spokes.
Deploying A2AServer Router
The `A2AServer` is the routing daemon that coordinates communication between agents. It acts as a local proxy, routes request envelopes, translates schemas, and handles message queue retries.
Step 1: Configure a2a.yaml Routing Manifest
Create an orchestration configuration file to specify routing rules, ports, and allowed agent keys:

blocks.code:
server:
  port: 8090
  host: "localhost"
  auth_token: "sec-key-9923"

routing:
  default_coordinator: "hub-agent"
  agents:
    - id: "hub-agent"
      port: 8091
    - id: "sql-agent"
      port: 8092
    - id: "git-agent"
      port: 8093

blocks.content: Step 2: Launch the routing server daemon
Start the server using the CLI commands to monitor incoming routes and connections:

blocks.title: Monitoring routes
blocks.content: A2AServer exposes a diagnostics panel on port 8090/health. You can inspect registered agent nodes, latency maps, and queue depths.
Check Your Understanding
Question 1: What is the primary role of the A2AServer routing daemon in an agent network?

- A) It executes local Python tests.
- B) It acts as a broker, routing request envelopes, managing message queues, and verifying authentication keys.
- C) It is a web interface for rendering HTML pages.
Feedback: Option B is correct. A2AServer manages message envelope exchanges, transport routes, security permissions, and connectivity states.
Remote Agents with RemoteA2aAgent
Not all agents can run on a single local server. The `RemoteA2aAgent` library allows you to bind an agent running on a remote cloud server to your local system as if it were a local module.
Writing a Python Remote Registration Script
Below is a python script utilizing the ADK library to spin up an agent and register it securely with a running `A2AServer` hub:

blocks.code:
from google_adk.a2a import RemoteA2aAgent, AgentServer

# Define the agent logic locally
def run_database_query(query: str) -> dict:
    # Database logic...
    return {"result": "records query success"}

# Initialize the remote agent configuration
remote_agent = RemoteA2aAgent(
    agent_id="sql-agent",
    server_url="http://localhost:8090",
    auth_token="sec-key-9923"
)

# Register capabilities and register tool functions
remote_agent.register_tool("query_db", run_database_query)

# Connect and listen for tasks from the orchestrator hub
if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("Starting RemoteA2aAgent loop for sql-agent...")
    remote_agent.start_event_loop()

blocks.content: Check Your Understanding
Question 1: When using RemoteA2aAgent, how does the remote agent receive tasks from the central hub?

- A) By writing logs to a shared network hard drive.
- B) It opens an event loop or long-poll WebSocket connection to the A2AServer router URL, awaiting task assignments.
- C) By scheduling cron jobs on the server.
Feedback: Option B is correct. RemoteA2aAgent establishes a persistent link to the routing hub server, listening for incoming events to execute.

modules.title: 4. Dynamic Discovery with Agent Cards and Manifests

blocks.content: Agent Discovery & Manifest Files
To enable dynamic task routing, the orchestrator needs to know what tools and operations an agent provides without hardcoding logic. This metadata is declared in the `agent-card.json` manifest file.
Agent Card Manifest Schema (`agent-card.json`)
Create an agent card manifest file that defines inputs, scopes, models, and contact endpoints for your agent:

blocks.title: Dynamic Routing Rules
blocks.content: The central hub parses all available card manifests in the directory. If a user asks to scan a repo for issues, the hub matches 'vulnerability-scan' to the scanner's card and routes the message.
Check Your Understanding
Question 1: What is the primary function of the agent-card.json file in a multi-agent system?

- A) It compiles Javascript dependencies.
- B) It acts as a declarative manifest describing an agent's capabilities, inputs, and endpoints to enable dynamic routing and discovery.
- C) It stores model weights for execution.
Feedback: Option B is correct. The agent-card.json provides the metadata required by routing engines to discover capabilities dynamically.

modules.title: 5. Lab - Implementing Collaborative Planning and Execution

blocks.content: Lab: Collaborative Agent Pipeline

In this lab, you will configure a cooperative multi-agent workflow: a Coordinator agent receives user instructions, plans actions, routes a scan request to a Security agent, and writes a compliance report file.
Step 1: Launch the A2AServer Daemon
Create the local router config and launch the central daemon:
Step 2: Start the Security Sub-Agent
Start the security script which runs a local scan tool and registers itself with the router daemon on port 8090:
Step 3: Trigger the Orchestration Task
Run the coordinator agent CLI command to execute the plan. The coordinator calls the registered security agent, waits for results, and prints reports:

blocks.code: adk run --coordinator --prompt "Scan the codebase folder src/app and output compliance results."

blocks.title: Trace Playback Verification
blocks.content: Open http://localhost:8501 to check step tracing. You should see a routing loop: Coordinator -> Security Agent (runs tool) -> Coordinator (compiles report).
